This appears to be a Chihuahuan Green Toad (Anaxyrus debilis), characterized by its vibrant green skin, small flattened body, and dark, wart-like spots.
These amphibians are native to arid regions in the southwestern United States and Mexico, often thriving near breeding pools after rain events.
They are primarily nocturnal, utilizing rodent burrows or cover under rocks to stay cool during the day.
